segments.text: Early in the morning, all the chief priests and the elders of the people made their plans how to have Jesus executed.
So they bound him, led him away and handed him over to Pilate the governor.
When Judas, who had betrayed him, saw that Jesus was condemned, he was seized with remorse and returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders.
I have sinned
segments.speaker: Judas

segments.text: he said,
for I have betrayed innocent blood.
segments.speaker: Judas

segments.text: They replied,
What is that to us? That's your responsibility.
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Elders

segments.text: So Judas threw the money into the temple and left. Then he went away and hanged himself.
Meanwhile Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked him,
Are you the king of the Jews?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: You have said so.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which of the two do you want me to release to you?
Barabbas
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: What shall I do, then, with Jesus who is called the Messiah?
Crucify him!
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: When Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere, but that instead an uproar was starting, he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd.
I am innocent of this man's blood. It is your responsibility!
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: His blood is on us and on our children!
segments.speaker: All the People

segments.text: Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani? (which means My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?)
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Surely he was the Son of God!
segments.speaker: Centurion and Guards

segments.text: Sir, we remember that while he was still alive that deceiver said, 'After three days I will rise again.'
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Pharisees
